title: pentacalia libertatis
side_effects: Pentacalia libertatis can have side effects if used improperly, such as nausea, dizziness, and allergic reactions, especially when ingested in large quantities. Prolonged use of the plant can lead to stomach ulcers and digestive problems, as well as an increased risk of bleeding due to its anticoagulant properties. In severe cases, improper use of pentacalia libertatis can cause seizures, coma, and even death. Additionally, topical application of the plant can result in skin irritation, rashes, and inflammation, particularly for individuals with sensitive skin.
